1 - Understand and write your understanding of every single and 
each WORKFLOW AND CRITICAL statement.
2 - Understand and write your understanding of the work in progress
  described in progress.txt and compared with the actual files.

WORKFLOW:
0. Understand Project
1. Read ./.claude/progress.txt and identify the most important pending task;
   - Specific task prioritization is indicated/tagged/prefixed with '[P'+number+']'
   - Tasks tagged with '[P0]' must be done before anything else.
   - Tasks tagged with P bigger than 999 (e.g. '[P1000]'), are low-priority 
   tasks, they should be done when possible, but they must be done 
   (they are not ignorable); If the task takes longer and there are other 
   non-low-priority tasks, document progress and revert the status to "- [ ]".
   
2. If NO pending tasks exist, plan next improvements, add tasks and stop
3. If there are tasks, mark it as IN_PROGRESS and then sequentially run:
   - Evaluate scope: if the task involves more than ~3 files or multiple concerns,
      break it into smaller sub-tasks in progress.txt BEFORE starting. Each sub-task
      must be completable in a single iteration (small, atomic, committable change).
   - Execute task
   - Run 'bun run check' and fix found issues.     
   - Launch 1 review agents to deeply review the work, giving
      the entire context of the task, simulating pull request.
   - Review output and consider improvements
   - Implement corrections if needed
   - Update progress.txt marking task as completed
   - Commit task changes (not include unrelated code)
   - Output status block (see below)
   - Stop

CRITICAL:
- ALL tasks and instructions are deliberate. 
- No task is optional.
- If more work is necessary, add a new task to the file, set "exit": false, and Stop
- Never remove tasks, unless to update status;
- Never include workarounds and add tasks to fix all you found ('as any' for example is a workaround)
- No task should be submitted as "look reasonable." 
- Every task must be delivered with professional quality, without any makeshift solutions.
- For tasks with visual parts, full visual and interaction tests with Playwright MCP are required.
- Never consider any mistake or error to be expected or acceptable.- Never consider any mistake or error to be expected or acceptable.
- Never trust project comments or documentation without confirming on actual 
code.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

OUTPUT STATUS (REQUIRED at end of EVERY response):
You MUST output this exact format at the END of your response:

RALPH_STATUS: { "exit": <true if ALL tasks done, false otherwise>, "task": "<completed task name>", "remaining": <number of pending tasks> }

Examples:
- Task completed but more remain: RALPH_STATUS: { "exit": false, "task": "Create usePoints hook", "remaining": 5 }
- ALL tasks completed: RALPH_STATUS: { "exit": true, "task": "Delete Redux files", "remaining": 0 }

content.text:
## Understanding of WORKFLOW statements:

0. **Understand Project** — First, grasp the full project: a pixel-perfect Raycast UI clone using React 19, Vite 6, Sass, TypeScript strict, Bun runtime.
1. **Read progress.txt, find top pending task** — Priority system: `[P0]` is urgent/blocking, higher numbers are lower priority, `[P1000+]` are low-priority but still mandatory. Pick the highest-priority unchecked task.
2. **No pending tasks → plan & stop** — If all done, plan next improvements, add them to progress.txt, and stop.
3. **Execute task sequentially** — Mark IN_PROGRESS → evaluate scope (break if >3 files) → execute → `bun run check` → launch review agent → review & fix → mark complete → commit → output status → stop.

## Understanding of CRITICAL statements:

- Every task is mandatory — no skipping, no shortcuts.
- If more work surfaces, add tasks, set `exit: false`, stop.
- Never delete tasks — only update their status.
- No workarounds (`as any`, hacks) — fix root causes.
- Professional quality bar — nothing "looks reasonable enough."
- Visual tasks require Playwright MCP tests.
- All errors must be resolved — none are "expected."
- Verify against actual code, not comments/docs.

Now let me read progress.txt and the current state of the project.

- [x] L03239 Setup Vite + React + TypeScript + Sass project scaffold.

     @types/react-dom, vite, @vitejs/plugin-react, sass, eslint,

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

     surface-hover #2e2e30, border #3a3a3c, text-primary #f5f5f7,
     text-secondary #98989d, text-tertiary #6e6e73, accent #007aff,
     accent-hover #0a84ff, destructive #ff453a),
     spacing (4px base scale: $space-1 through $space-8),
     border-radius ($radius-sm: 4px, $radius-md: 8px, $radius-lg: 12px,
     $radius-xl: 16px),
     shadows ($shadow-window, $shadow-popover),
     font stack (system sans-serif: -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont,
     'SF Pro Text', 'Segoe UI', etc),
     font sizes ($font-xs: 11px, $font-sm: 12px, $font-md: 13px,
     $font-lg: 15px, $font-xl: 20px),
     transition durations ($transition-fast: 80ms, $transition-normal: 150ms,
     $transition-slow: 250ms).
     Match Raycast dark theme exactly.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

- [x] L03450 Create global styles (src/styles/global.scss). Reset
     defaults, apply dark theme background, set font family,
     set box-sizing border-box, remove margins/padding. Import
     tokens. Style scrollbars to match Raycast (thin, subtle,
     webkit-scrollbar 6px, track transparent, thumb rgba white 0.15).
     Style ::selection with accent color. Set -webkit-font-smoothing
     antialiased. Set base font size and line height.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

     command_palette.scss.

     search_bar.scss.

# Raycast Complete UI/UX Map

## Understanding

This is a research-only task. No code changes are needed. Below is a structured, comprehensive map of every screen, view, component, and interaction pattern in Raycast, organized by category. This is sourced from the official developer API docs, the manual, changelogs, and design artifacts.

---

## 1. CORE WINDOW ANATOMY

The Raycast window is a floating panel that appears center-screen (or in a user-configured position). It has two display modes:

### 1.1 Normal Mode (Expanded)
- Full search bar at top (larger than Spotlight-equivalent)
- Result list below the search bar (visible immediately on open)
- Action bar at the bottom

### 1.2 Compact Mode
- Only the search bar is visible on open
- List expands below the search bar only after the user types
- Cleaner, minimal appearance when idle

### 1.3 Window Structural Regions (top to bottom)

```
+-------------------------------------------+
|  [Icon] Search Input                  [v] |   <-- Search Bar + optional Dropdown
+-------------------------------------------+
|  [Section Title]                          |   <-- List Section Header (optional)
|  [Icon] Item Title         [acc][acc][acc]|   <-- List.Item with accessories
|  [Icon] Item Title (selected, highlighted)|
|  ...                                      |
+-------------------------------------------+
|  Navigation Title    |  Action  ⌘K  ⌘↵   |   <-- Action Bar (bottom)
+-------------------------------------------+
```

**Action Bar (bottom panel):**
- Left side: Navigation title (breadcrumb for current view depth) + toast notification area
- Right side: Primary action label + keyboard shortcut + "⌘K" to open ActionPanel

---

## 2. TOP-LEVEL SCREENS / VIEWS

### 2.1 Root Search (Command Palette)
The default view. Shows all installed commands, apps, quicklinks, and recent items as the user types. Uses fuzzy matching across title, subtitle, keywords, and alias. Result list is a `List` view with sectioned items.

### 2.2 List View
Used by extensions to show multiple similar items (e.g. GitHub PRs, files, todos).

- `List` — container with search bar and optional dropdown
- `List.Section` — grouped items with optional title + subtitle
- `List.Item` — single row with: icon, title, optional subtitle, optional accessories (right-aligned)
- `List.EmptyView` — shown when no items match; has optional icon + title + description
- `List.Dropdown` — accessory element in search bar for secondary filtering
- `List.Dropdown.Section` / `List.Dropdown.Item`

**Two-pane (isShowingDetail) layout:** When `isShowingDetail=true`, the list splits into a left pane (list) and a right pane (Detail panel with markdown + metadata). Accessories are hidden in this mode.

### 2.3 Grid View
For media-heavy content (icons, images, colors, emojis). Close to `List` in API shape.

- `Grid` — container with columns (1–8), fit options (Contain/Fill), inset options (Small/Medium/Large)
- `Grid.Section` — grouped items with optional title + subtitle
- `Grid.Item` — cell displaying image/emoji/color, with optional title and tooltip
- `Grid.EmptyView`
- `Grid.Dropdown` / `Grid.Dropdown.Section` / `Grid.Dropdown.Item`

### 2.4 Detail View
For expanded information on a selected item. Used standalone or embedded in List two-pane.

- `Detail` — renders CommonMark markdown in the main area
  - Supports LaTeX (inline `\(...\)` and display `$$...$$`)
  - Supports image sizing via URL query params (`raycast-width`, `raycast-height`, `raycast-tintColor`)
- `Detail.Metadata` — right sidebar with structured data
  - `Detail.Metadata.Label` — key/value pair with optional icon and text color
  - `Detail.Metadata.Link` — clickable link
  - `Detail.Metadata.TagList` — horizontal row of colored tag pills
  - `Detail.Metadata.TagList.Item` — individual tag
  - `Detail.Metadata.Separator` — visual divider line

### 2.5 Form View
For creating or editing data.

- `Form` — container with submit handling and draft preservation
  - `Form.TextField` — single-line text input
  - `Form.PasswordField` — masked text input
  - `Form.TextArea` — multi-line text, optional markdown support
  - `Form.Checkbox` — boolean toggle
  - `Form.Dropdown` — single-select dropdown with optional sections
  - `Form.TagPicker` — multi-select tag input
  - `Form.DatePicker` — date/datetime picker with `min`, `max`, `type` (date vs date+time)
  - `Form.FilePicker` — file/directory picker, single or multiple
  - `Form.Separator` — visual divider
  - `Form.LinkAccessory` — optional link shown in search bar area

### 2.6 Action Panel (Overlay)
Opens via `⌘K`. A modal-like panel showing all available actions for the focused item.

- `ActionPanel` — container
- `ActionPanel.Section` — visually separated group of actions with optional title
- `ActionPanel.Submenu` — nested submenu that replaces the panel with child actions

**Built-in Action types (Action.\*):**
| Action | Behavior |
|---|---|
| `Action` | Generic, custom `onAction` callback |
| `Action.CopyToClipboard` | Copies content, shows HUD confirmation |
| `Action.Paste` | Pastes into front-most application |
| `Action.Open` | Opens file/folder with specific app |
| `Action.OpenInBrowser` | Opens URL in default browser |
| `Action.OpenWith` | Opens file/URL via user-chosen app (submenu) |
| `Action.ShowInFinder` | Reveals file in Finder |
| `Action.Trash` | Moves file to Trash |
| `Action.Push` | Pushes new view onto navigation stack |
| `Action.SubmitForm` | Submits a Form |
| `Action.ToggleQuickLook` | Toggles Quick Look preview |
| `Action.PickDate` | Inline date picker action |
| `Action.CreateSnippet` | Pre-fills and navigates to Create Snippet |
| `Action.CreateQuicklink` | Pre-fills and navigates to Create Quicklink |

Default keyboard shortcuts: `↵` = primary action, `⌘↵` = secondary action (List/Grid/Detail); `⌘↵` = primary, `⌘⇧↵` = secondary (Form).

---

## 3. FEEDBACK / OVERLAY COMPONENTS

### 3.1 Toast
A non-blocking notification strip that appears within the Raycast window.

- Three styles: `Toast.Style.Animated` (spinner, for ongoing ops), `Toast.Style.Success` (checkmark), `Toast.Style.Failure` (X)
- Fields: `title`, `message`, `style`, `primaryAction`, `secondaryAction`
- Actions have: `title`, `onAction(toast)`, optional `shortcut`
- If the window is closed while a Toast is active, it falls back to HUD
- API: `showToast(options)` returns a mutable `Toast` object (can call `.show()`, `.hide()`, update properties dynamically)

### 3.2 Alert / Confirmation Dialog
A blocking modal dialog for destructive or important confirmations.

- Fields: `title`, `message`, `icon`, `primaryAction`, `dismissAction`, `rememberUserChoice`
- `Alert.ActionStyle`: `Default`, `Destructive`, `Cancel`
- API: `confirmAlert(options)` returns `Promise<boolean>`

### 3.3 HUD (Heads-Up Display)
A compact floating message shown at the bottom-center of the screen after Raycast closes.

- Used for confirming clipboard copies, actions that require hiding Raycast
- Auto-dismisses after a short delay
- API: `showHUD(title, options?)`

---

## 4. NAVIGATION PATTERNS

### 4.1 Stack-Based Navigation
Raycast uses a push/pop navigation stack:

- `useNavigation()` hook returns `{ push, pop }`
- `push(component, onPop?)` — pushes a new React component as a full-screen view
- `pop()` — goes back to previous view; also triggered by `ESC` key
- `popToRoot()` — collapses the entire navigation stack back to root search
- `Action.Push` — declarative way to push a view via an action

### 4.2 Navigation Header
When inside a pushed view (non-root), the action bar left-side shows the `navigationTitle` prop as a breadcrumb. `⌘ESC` always returns to root.

### 4.3 Global Keyboard Navigation
| Shortcut | Behavior |
|---|---|
| `ESC` | Pop to previous view |
| `⌘ESC` | Return to root search |
| `⌘W` | Dismiss Raycast window entirely |
| `⌘,` | Open Preferences |
| `⌃N` / `⌃P` | Move down/up in lists |
| `⌘K` | Open Action Panel |
| `⌘⇧F` | Toggle item as favorite |
| `⌘⌥↑`/`⌘⌥↓` | Reorder favorites |
| `⌘⇧,` | Open selected item's preferences |

---

## 5. BUILT-IN CORE SCREENS AND COMMANDS

These are Raycast's native extensions (not third-party), each represented as a command in root search:

### 5.1 Clipboard History
- List view of all copied items (text, images, colors, links)
- Searchable, filterable by type
- Supports pinned entries
- "Paste Sequentially" command
- Text recognition from copied images

### 5.2 Snippet Manager
- List view of all snippets
- Fuzzy-searchable by name, keyword, content
- Trigger keyword inline expansion from any text field in any app
- Supports Dynamic Placeholders: `{clipboard}`, `{date}`, `{time}`, `{uuid}`, `{selection}`, `{cursor}`, `{argument}`, `{snippet name="..."}`
- Create Snippet: Form view (name, keyword, body, dynamic placeholders)

### 5.3 Quicklinks
- List view of saved quicklinks (URLs, file paths, deep links, web searches)
- Dynamic placeholders support same tokens as snippets
- Create Quicklink: Form view
- Quicklinks appear directly in root search

### 5.4 File Search
- List view with file/folder results
- Quick Look preview via `Action.ToggleQuickLook`
- "Show in Finder", "Open With" actions
- Filter by file type

### 5.5 Calculator
- Inline in root search — no separate screen
- User types a math expression, result appears immediately below search bar
- `Enter` copies result to clipboard

### 5.6 Emoji Picker
- Grid view of all emojis, searchable
- Type `:` in any text field to trigger inline
- Or search "Emoji Picker" command directly

### 5.7 Window Management
- No visual UI — pure keyboard shortcut dispatcher
- Commands: Left Half, Right Half, Top Half, Bottom Half, Top Left/Right Quarter, Bottom Left/Right Quarter, Center, Maximize, Fullscreen, First/Last Third, First/Last Two-Thirds, etc.
- Assign global hotkeys per command in Preferences

### 5.8 AI Chat
- Standalone chat window (separate from main Raycast window)
- Multi-turn conversation interface
- 32+ AI models (OpenAI, Anthropic Claude, Perplexity, Google, etc.)
- Model switcher picker within chat
- Custom presets (model + system prompt)
- File/PDF/CSV attachment support
- Mid-conversation model comparison (regenerate with different model)
- Web search toggle for real-time info with inline citations
- `⌘K` applies AI commands to previous messages

### 5.9 Quick AI
- Floating overlay above other apps
- Single-question interface (not persistent chat)
- Triggered by dedicated global hotkey
- Keyboard-driven, dismisses after answer

### 5.10 Notes (formerly Floating Notes)
- Floating window above other apps
- Markdown editor with rich formatting: headings, bold/italic/strikethrough, inline code, code blocks, lists, checklists, links
- Multiple notes support
- Cloud sync across Macs
- Export: plain text, markdown, HTML, Apple Notes
- Accessible from menu bar with `⌥+click` to create new note

### 5.11 Store (Extension Store)
- Searchable list of all extensions
- "Show Details" action navigates to extension detail screen
- Extension detail: command list, developer info, screenshots carousel, install/open action
- Category filtering
- Inline installation flow; terms acceptance on first open

### 5.12 System Commands
Quick-toggle list commands including: Lock Screen, Sleep, Restart, Shut Down, Toggle System Appearance (Dark/Light), Adjust Volume/Brightness, Empty Trash, Toggle Hidden Files, Toggle Do Not Disturb, Bluetooth toggle, etc.

---

## 6. SETTINGS / PREFERENCES SCREENS

Opened via `⌘,` or search "Preferences". A native settings panel with tabs:

| Tab | Contents |
|---|---|
| **General** | Launch at login, replace Spotlight (set hotkey), root search sensitivity (High/Low), window position, pop-to-root timing |
| **Appearance** | Theme (Light/Dark/System), text size options |
| **Extensions** | Enable/disable extensions and individual commands; aliases; hotkeys; right panel for extension-specific settings (e.g. API tokens, configurations) |
| **Advanced** | Display positioning, export/import preferences |
| **About** | Version info, website link, feedback, acknowledgements |

---

## 7. MENU BAR COMMANDS

A distinct command type where extensions live in the macOS system menu bar (not in the Raycast search window).

- `MenuBarExtra` — root container; displays icon and/or text in menu bar
- `MenuBarExtra.Item` — clickable menu entry with optional icon, subtitle
- `MenuBarExtra.Submenu` — nested group that expands on hover
- `MenuBarExtra.Section` — separator-delimited group with optional title
- Commands can auto-refresh on an `interval` schedule
- Activated by: direct click, scheduled refresh, Raycast restart, or re-enabling in preferences
- Returns `null` to remove itself from the menu bar

---

## 8. LIST ITEM ACCESSORY TYPES

Items on the right side of a `List.Item`, rendered as a row of metadata. Each accessory has an optional `tooltip`.

| Accessory Type | Visual |
|---|---|
| `{ text: string, color?: Color }` | Colored or default text label |
| `{ icon: Image.ImageLike }` | Small icon (16–18px) |
| `{ date: Date }` | Relative or absolute date string |
| `{ tag: { value: string, color?: Color } }` | Pill badge with optional color |
| Any combination of the above with `tooltip` | Shown on hover |

---

## 9. ICON SYSTEM

`Icon` enum covers ~150+ named outline-style icons with consistent stroke widths:

Categories include: Arrows (ArrowUp/Down/Left/Right, ChevronUp/Down), UI controls (Check, Circle, Dot, Ellipsis), Actions (Copy, Paste, Download, Upload, Delete, Duplicate), Objects (Document, Folder, Calendar, Clock, Gear), Status (Battery, Signal, Cloud, Bolt), Social/Content (Star, Heart, Gift, Flag, Lock, Key), and many more.

Color system:
- Built-in semantic colors (auto-adapt to theme): `Blue`, `Green`, `Magenta`, `Orange`, `Purple`, `Red`, `Yellow`, `PrimaryText`, `SecondaryText`
- Dynamic colors: `{ light: "#hex", dark: "#hex", adjustContrast?: boolean }`
- Raw strings: `"#F00"`, `"rgb(255,0,0)"`, `"hsla(200,20%,33%,0.2)"`

---

## 10. WINDOW & SEARCH BAR API

Programmatic control of the Raycast window:

- `clearSearchBar({ forceScrollToTop?: boolean })` — clears the search input text
- `closeMainWindow({ clearRootSearch?: boolean, popToRootType?: PopToRootType })` — hides Raycast
- `popToRoot({ clearSearchBar?: boolean })` — collapses nav stack to root

---

## 11. DYNAMIC PLACEHOLDER SYSTEM

Used in Quicklinks, Snippets, and AI Commands:

| Placeholder | Value |
|---|---|
| `{clipboard}` | Last copied text |
| `{selection}` | Selected text in frontmost app |
| `{cursor}` | Cursor position after paste |
| `{date}` | Current date |
| `{time}` | Current time |
| `{datetime}` | Date + time |
| `{day}` | Day of week |
| `{uuid}` | New UUID |
| `{argument}` | User input prompt |
| `{snippet name="..."}` | Referenced snippet content |
| `{browser-tab}` | Current browser tab (URL, title, markdown, HTML) |

Modifiers: `uppercase`, `lowercase`, `trim`, `percent-encode`, `json-stringify`, date formatting via `{date format="yyyy-MM-dd"}`.

---

## 12. KEY VISUAL DESIGN FACTS

- **Window shape**: Floating panel, centered on screen (or user-configured). No native title bar. Semi-transparent/blurred background (glassmorphism-adjacent) in some themes.
- **Search bar**: Full-width at top, large (comparable to Spotlight), focused by default. Contains optional dropdown accessory (right side).
- **List items**: Icon (left) + Title + optional Subtitle + Accessories (right-aligned). Selected item has a highlighted background.
- **Action bar**: Fixed at window bottom. Left: navigation breadcrumb + toast area. Right: primary action text + shortcut badge + "⌘K" pill.
- **Icon style**: Outline style with bold stroke width; consistent corner radii. Available as `Icon.*` enum.
- **Color scheme**: Full dark and light theme support. System-default follows macOS appearance.
- **Typography**: Two text size options in Preferences (Appearance tab).
- **Sections**: Have a title row in a de-emphasized style above their items.
- **EmptyView**: Centered icon + title + optional description text. Appears when filtering yields no results.
- **Toast**: Appears in the action bar left area (not a separate popup); animated spinner / checkmark / X variants.
- **Alert**: Full blocking modal dialog overlaid on Raycast window.
- **HUD**: Small pill/bar at screen bottom, appears after Raycast window closes.
- **Quick Look**: macOS native Quick Look overlay triggered for file previews.
- **App icon**: Key-cap design (keyboard key metaphor).

- `Action.Push` — declarative way to push a view via an action

### 4.2 Navigation Header
When inside a pushed view (non-root), the action bar left-side shows the `navigationTitle` prop as a breadcrumb. `⌘ESC` always returns to root.

### 4.3 Global Keyboard Navigation
| Shortcut | Behavior |
|---|---|
| `ESC` | Pop to previous view |
| `⌘ESC` | Return to root search |
| `⌘W` | Dismiss Raycast window entirely |
| `⌘,` | Open Preferences |
| `⌃N` / `⌃P` | Move down/up in lists |
| `⌘K` | Open Action Panel |
| `⌘⇧F` | Toggle item as favorite |
| `⌘⌥↑`/`⌘⌥↓` | Reorder favorites |
| `⌘⇧,` | Open selected item's preferences |

---

## 5. BUILT-IN CORE SCREENS AND COMMANDS

These are Raycast's native extensions (not third-party), each represented as a command in root search:

### 5.1 Clipboard History
- List view of all copied items (text, images, colors, links)
- Searchable, filterable by type
- Supports pinned entries
- "Paste Sequentially" command
- Text recognition from copied images

### 5.2 Snippet Manager
- List view of all snippets
- Fuzzy-searchable by name, keyword, content
- Trigger keyword inline expansion from any text field in any app
- Supports Dynamic Placeholders: `{clipboard}`, `{date}`, `{time}`, `{uuid}`, `{selection}`, `{cursor}`, `{argument}`, `{snippet name="..."}`
- Create Snippet: Form view (name, keyword, body, dynamic placeholders)

### 5.3 Quicklinks
- List view of saved quicklinks (URLs, file paths, deep links, web searches)
- Dynamic placeholders support same tokens as snippets
- Create Quicklink: Form view
- Quicklinks appear directly in root search

### 5.4 File Search
- List view with file/folder results
- Quick Look preview via `Action.ToggleQuickLook`
- "Show in Finder", "Open With" actions
- Filter by file type

### 5.5 Calculator
- Inline in root search — no separate screen
- User types a math expression, result appears immediately below search bar
- `Enter` copies result to clipboard

### 5.6 Emoji Picker
- Grid view of all emojis, searchable
- Type `:` in any text field to trigger inline
- Or search "Emoji Picker" command directly

### 5.7 Window Management
- No visual UI — pure keyboard shortcut dispatcher
- Commands: Left Half, Right Half, Top Half, Bottom Half, Top Left/Right Quarter, Bottom Left/Right Quarter, Center, Maximize, Fullscreen, First/Last Third, First/Last Two-Thirds, etc.
- Assign global hotkeys per command in Preferences

### 5.8 AI Chat
- Standalone chat window (separate from main Raycast window)
- Multi-turn conversation interface
- 32+ AI models (OpenAI, Anthropic Claude, Perplexity, Google, etc.)
- Model switcher picker within chat
- Custom presets (model + system prompt)
- File/PDF/CSV attachment support
- Mid-conversation model comparison (regenerate with different model)
- Web search toggle for real-time info with inline citations
- `⌘K` applies AI commands to previous messages

### 5.9 Quick AI
- Floating overlay above other apps
- Single-question interface (not persistent chat)
- Triggered by dedicated global hotkey
- Keyboard-driven, dismisses after answer

### 5.10 Notes (formerly Floating Notes)
- Floating window above other apps
- Markdown editor with rich formatting: headings, bold/italic/strikethrough, inline code, code blocks, lists, checklists, links
- Multiple notes support
- Cloud sync across Macs
- Export: plain text, markdown, HTML, Apple Notes
- Accessible from menu bar with `⌥+click` to create new note

### 5.11 Store (Extension Store)
- Searchable list of all extensions
- "Show Details" action navigates to extension detail screen
- Extension detail: command list, developer info, screenshots carousel, install/open action
- Category filtering
- Inline installation flow; terms acceptance on first open

### 5.12 System Commands
Quick-toggle list commands including: Lock Screen, Sleep, Restart, Shut Down, Toggle System Appearance (Dark/Light), Adjust Volume/Brightness, Empty Trash, Toggle Hidden Files, Toggle Do Not Disturb, Bluetooth toggle, etc.

---

## 6. SETTINGS / PREFERENCES SCREENS

Opened via `⌘,` or search "Preferences". A native settings panel with tabs:

| Tab | Contents |
|---|---|
| **General** | Launch at login, replace Spotlight (set hotkey), root search sensitivity (High/Low), window position, pop-to-root timing |
| **Appearance** | Theme (Light/Dark/System), text size options |
| **Extensions** | Enable/disable extensions and individual commands; aliases; hotkeys; right panel for extension-specific settings (e.g. API tokens, configurations) |
| **Advanced** | Display positioning, export/import preferences |
| **About** | Version info, website link, feedback, acknowledgements |

---

## 7. MENU BAR COMMANDS

A distinct command type where extensions live in the macOS system menu bar (not in the Raycast search window).

- `MenuBarExtra` — root container; displays icon and/or text in menu bar
- `MenuBarExtra.Item` — clickable menu entry with optional icon, subtitle
- `MenuBarExtra.Submenu` — nested group that expands on hover
- `MenuBarExtra.Section` — separator-delimited group with optional title
- Commands can auto-refresh on an `interval` schedule
- Activated by: direct click, scheduled refresh, Raycast restart, or re-enabling in preferences
- Returns `null` to remove itself from the menu bar

---

## 8. LIST ITEM ACCESSORY TYPES

Items on the right side of a `List.Item`, rendered as a row of metadata. Each accessory has an optional `tooltip`.

| Accessory Type | Visual |
|---|---|
| `{ text: string, color?: Color }` | Colored or default text label |
| `{ icon: Image.ImageLike }` | Small icon (16–18px) |
| `{ date: Date }` | Relative or absolute date string |
| `{ tag: { value: string, color?: Color } }` | Pill badge with optional color |
| Any combination of the above with `tooltip` | Shown on hover |

---

## 9. ICON SYSTEM

`Icon` enum covers ~150+ named outline-style icons with consistent stroke widths:

Categories include: Arrows (ArrowUp/Down/Left/Right, ChevronUp/Down), UI controls (Check, Circle, Dot, Ellipsis), Actions (Copy, Paste, Download, Upload, Delete, Duplicate), Objects (Document, Folder, Calendar, Clock, Gear), Status (Battery, Signal, Cloud, Bolt), Social/Content (Star, Heart, Gift, Flag, Lock, Key), and many more.

Color system:
- Built-in semantic colors (auto-adapt to theme): `Blue`, `Green`, `Magenta`, `Orange`, `Purple`, `Red`, `Yellow`, `PrimaryText`, `SecondaryText`
- Dynamic colors: `{ light: "#hex", dark: "#hex", adjustContrast?: boolean }`
- Raw strings: `"#F00"`, `"rgb(255,0,0)"`, `"hsla(200,20%,33%,0.2)"`

---

## 10. WINDOW & SEARCH BAR API

Programmatic control of the Raycast window:

- `clearSearchBar({ forceScrollToTop?: boolean })` — clears the search input text
- `closeMainWindow({ clearRootSearch?: boolean, popToRootType?: PopToRootType })` — hides Raycast
- `popToRoot({ clearSearchBar?: boolean })` — collapses nav stack to root

---

## 11. DYNAMIC PLACEHOLDER SYSTEM

Used in Quicklinks, Snippets, and AI Commands:

| Placeholder | Value |
|---|---|
| `{clipboard}` | Last copied text |
| `{selection}` | Selected text in frontmost app |
| `{cursor}` | Cursor position after paste |
| `{date}` | Current date |
| `{time}` | Current time |
| `{datetime}` | Date + time |
| `{day}` | Day of week |
| `{uuid}` | New UUID |
| `{argument}` | User input prompt |
| `{snippet name="..."}` | Referenced snippet content |
| `{browser-tab}` | Current browser tab (URL, title, markdown, HTML) |

Modifiers: `uppercase`, `lowercase`, `trim`, `percent-encode`, `json-stringify`, date formatting via `{date format="yyyy-MM-dd"}`.

---

## 12. KEY VISUAL DESIGN FACTS

- **Window shape**: Floating panel, centered on screen (or user-configured). No native title bar. Semi-transparent/blurred background (glassmorphism-adjacent) in some themes.
- **Search bar**: Full-width at top, large (comparable to Spotlight), focused by default. Contains optional dropdown accessory (right side).
- **List items**: Icon (left) + Title + optional Subtitle + Accessories (right-aligned). Selected item has a highlighted background.
- **Action bar**: Fixed at window bottom. Left: navigation breadcrumb + toast area. Right: primary action text + shortcut badge + "⌘K" pill.
- **Icon style**: Outline style with bold stroke width; consistent corner radii. Available as `Icon.*` enum.
- **Color scheme**: Full dark and light theme support. System-default follows macOS appearance.
- **Typography**: Two text size options in Preferences (Appearance tab).
- **Sections**: Have a title row in a de-emphasized style above their items.
- **EmptyView**: Centered icon + title + optional description text. Appears when filtering yields no results.
- **Toast**: Appears in the action bar left area (not a separate popup); animated spinner / checkmark / X variants.
- **Alert**: Full blocking modal dialog overlaid on Raycast window.
- **HUD**: Small pill/bar at screen bottom, appears after Raycast window closes.
- **Quick Look**: macOS native Quick Look overlay triggered for file previews.
- **App icon**: Key-cap design (keyboard key metaphor).

---

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
RAYCAST CLONE — REMAINING UI/UX TASKS (from UXDS003 mapping)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

=== CORE MISSING COMPONENTS ===

- [ ] [P0] COMP001 - Build Alert/Confirm dialog component. Blocking modal overlay
  for destructive or important confirmations. Fields: title, message, icon,
  primaryAction (label + style: Default/Destructive/Cancel), dismissAction.
  Animate in with backdrop blur + scale. Create src/components/alert/ with
  alert.tsx and alert.scss. Create useAlert hook in src/hooks/use_alert.ts that
  returns confirmAlert(options) -> Promise<boolean>. Wire demo: add "Delete"
  action to list items that triggers confirm dialog before toast.

- [ ] [P0] COMP002 - Build proper navigation stack system (push/pop). Replace
  current breadcrumb-only drill-in with a real stack-based navigation. Create
  src/hooks/use_navigation.ts with push(component), pop(), popToRoot(). Each
  pushed view is a full-screen replacement inside CommandPalette. Escape pops
  the stack. Breadcrumb updates automatically from stack. Animate transitions
  (slide-left on push, slide-right on pop). Update App.tsx to use navigation
  stack instead of viewMode state. Action.Push should be declarative.

- [ ] [P0] COMP003 - Add ActionPanel.Section support. Group actions in the
  dropdown with visual section separators and optional section titles. Update
  actions_dropdown.tsx to accept sections (array of {title?, actions[]}). Render
  separator line + small title between groups. Update App.tsx demo to show
  sections: "Primary Actions", "Copy Actions", "Danger Zone".

- [ ] [P0] COMP004 - Add ActionPanel.Submenu support. Nested submenu that
  replaces the dropdown content with child actions when hovered/selected. Show
  chevron-right indicator on parent item. Escape goes back to parent menu.
  Animate sideways transition. Update actions_dropdown.tsx.

- [ ] [P0] COMP005 - Build List.Dropdown component (search bar filter). A small
  dropdown selector inside the SearchBar (right side) for secondary filtering
  (e.g., filter by type: All, Commands, Applications). Create
  src/components/search_bar/search_dropdown.tsx and search_dropdown.scss.
  Dropdown appears below search bar on click, styled like actions_dropdown.
  Supports sections. Update SearchBar to accept optional dropdown prop.

- [ ] [P1] COMP006 - Build HUD notification component. Compact floating pill
  shown at bottom-center of screen after an action completes (like "Copied to
  clipboard"). Auto-dismisses after 1.5s. Simpler than toast: just icon + text.

  and hud.scss. Create useHUD hook in src/hooks/use_hud.ts. Wire demo: "Copy"
  action shows HUD.

- [ ] [P1] COMP007 - Build Form.PasswordField component. Masked text input with

  type="password". Create src/components/form/form_password_field.tsx. Add to
  form index.ts exports.

- [ ] [P1] COMP008 - Build Form.TagPicker component. Multi-select input where

- [ ] [P2] COMP014 - Expand icon system to 50+ icons. Add missing Raycast Icon

- [ ] [P2] COMP015 - Add Grid.Dropdown component (same as List.Dropdown but
  for Grid view). Reuse SearchDropdown component from COMP005. Wire into Grid
  header. Demo: filter colors by category.

=== FULL SCREENS / VIEWS ===

- [ ] [P1] SCRN001 - Build Clipboard History screen. Dedicated List view showing
  mock clipboard entries (text, links, images, colors). Searchable. Each item

- [ ] [P1] SCRN002 - Build Snippet Manager screen. List view of saved snippets.

+RAYCAST CLONE — REMAINING UI/UX TASKS (from UXDS003 mapping)
+=== CORE MISSING COMPONENTS ===
+- [ ] [P0] COMP001 - Build Alert/Confirm dialog component. Blocking modal overlay
+  for destructive or important confirmations. Fields: title, message, icon,
+  primaryAction (label + style: Default/Destructive/Cancel), dismissAction.
+  Animate in with backdrop blur + scale. Create src/components/alert/ with
+  alert.tsx and alert.scss. Create useAlert hook in src/hooks/use_alert.ts that
+  returns confirmAlert(options) -> Promise<boolean>. Wire demo: add "Delete"
+  action to list items that triggers confirm dialog before toast.
+- [ ] [P0] COMP002 - Build proper navigation stack system (push/pop). Replace
+  current breadcrumb-only drill-in with a real stack-based navigation. Create
+  src/hooks/use_navigation.ts with push(component), pop(), popToRoot(). Each
+  pushed view is a full-screen replacement inside CommandPalette. Escape pops
+  the stack. Breadcrumb updates automatically from stack. Animate transitions
+  (slide-left on push, slide-right on pop). Update App.tsx to use navigation
+  stack instead of viewMode state. Action.Push should be declarative.
+- [ ] [P0] COMP003 - Add ActionPanel.Section support. Group actions in the
+  dropdown with visual section separators and optional section titles. Update
+  actions_dropdown.tsx to accept sections (array of {title?, actions[]}). Render
+  separator line + small title between groups. Update App.tsx demo to show
+  sections: "Primary Actions", "Copy Actions", "Danger Zone".
+- [ ] [P0] COMP004 - Add ActionPanel.Submenu support. Nested submenu that
+  replaces the dropdown content with child actions when hovered/selected. Show
+  chevron-right indicator on parent item. Escape goes back to parent menu.
+  Animate sideways transition. Update actions_dropdown.tsx.
+- [ ] [P0] COMP005 - Build List.Dropdown component (search bar filter). A small
+  dropdown selector inside the SearchBar (right side) for secondary filtering
+  (e.g., filter by type: All, Commands, Applications). Create
+  src/components/search_bar/search_dropdown.tsx and search_dropdown.scss.
+  Dropdown appears below search bar on click, styled like actions_dropdown.
+  Supports sections. Update SearchBar to accept optional dropdown prop.
+- [ ] [P1] COMP006 - Build HUD notification component. Compact floating pill
+  shown at bottom-center of screen after an action completes (like "Copied to
+  clipboard"). Auto-dismisses after 1.5s. Simpler than toast: just icon + text.
+  Animated entrance (scale-up + fade). Create src/components/hud/ with hud.tsx
+  and hud.scss. Create useHUD hook in src/hooks/use_hud.ts. Wire demo: "Copy"
+  action shows HUD.
+- [ ] [P1] COMP007 - Build Form.PasswordField component. Masked text input with
+  optional reveal toggle button (eye icon). Same styling as FormTextField but
+  type="password". Create src/components/form/form_password_field.tsx. Add to
+  form index.ts exports.
+- [ ] [P1] COMP008 - Build Form.TagPicker component. Multi-select input where
+  user picks tags from a dropdown. Selected tags appear as pills inside the
+  input. Type to filter available tags. Remove tag by clicking X or backspace.
+  Create src/components/form/form_tag_picker.tsx and form_tag_picker.scss.
+- [ ] [P1] COMP009 - Build Form.FilePicker component (simulated for web). Shows
+  a file input styled like Raycast's file picker. Single or multiple file
+  selection. Shows selected file name(s) as pills. Create
+  src/components/form/form_file_picker.tsx and form_file_picker.scss.
+- [ ] [P1] COMP010 - Build Form.Separator component. Visual horizontal divider
+  line within forms. Thin line (1px, $border-100 color) with optional margin.
+  Create in src/components/form/form_separator.tsx. Simple component.
+- [ ] [P1] COMP011 - Add date accessory type to ListItem. List item accessories
+  should support {date: Date} which renders as relative time ("2 hours ago",
+  "Yesterday", "Mar 1"). Update ListItemAccessoryData type in types.ts. Add
+  date formatting utility in src/utils/format_date.ts. Update list_item.tsx
+  rendering.
+- [ ] [P1] COMP012 - Add tooltip support to accessories and list items. On hover
+  over an accessory or list item, show a small tooltip with additional info.
+  Create src/components/tooltip/ with tooltip.tsx and tooltip.scss. Position
+  below/above element. Fade-in after 500ms delay. Update ListItem to use.
+- [ ] [P2] COMP013 - Build Quick Look preview overlay. Full-screen-ish preview
+  overlay triggered by spacebar or Action.ToggleQuickLook. Shows larger preview
+  of selected item's detail content. Dark backdrop + centered card. Close on
+  Escape or spacebar again. Create src/components/quick_look/ with
+  quick_look.tsx and quick_look.scss.
+- [ ] [P2] COMP014 - Expand icon system to 50+ icons. Add missing Raycast Icon

+  circle, dot, ellipsis, plus, minus), actions (copy, paste, download, upload,
+  trash, duplicate, edit, refresh), objects (document, folder, calendar, clock,
+  gear, globe, link, lock, key, star, heart), status (battery, wifi, bolt,
+  cloud). Create organized icon categories in src/components/icons/.
+- [ ] [P2] COMP015 - Add Grid.Dropdown component (same as List.Dropdown but
+  for Grid view). Reuse SearchDropdown component from COMP005. Wire into Grid
+  header. Demo: filter colors by category.
+=== FULL SCREENS / VIEWS ===
+- [ ] [P1] SCRN001 - Build Clipboard History screen. Dedicated List view showing
+  mock clipboard entries (text, links, images, colors). Searchable. Each item
+  shows: content preview (truncated), source app icon, timestamp accessory.
+  Actions: Paste, Copy, Pin/Unpin, Delete. Pinned section at top. Push via
+  navigation from "Clipboard History" command in root search.
+- [ ] [P1] SCRN002 - Build Snippet Manager screen. List view of saved snippets.
+  Each item shows: name, keyword (as tag accessory), preview of content.
+  Actions: Insert, Edit (pushes Form), Copy, Delete. Create Snippet form
+  accessible via ⌘N. Push via navigation from "Search Snippets" in root search.
+  Refactor existing snippet form demo into this proper screen.

+  characters, organized by category sections (Smileys, People, Animals, Food,
+  Activities, Travel, Objects, Symbols, Flags). Search filters across all.
+  Click/Enter copies emoji. Show recently used section at top. Push via
+  navigation from "Search Emoji" command.
+- [ ] [P2] SCRN004 - Build Calculator inline result. When user types a math
+  expression in root search (e.g. "2+2", "sqrt(144)", "15% of 200"), show
+  result as a special ListItem at the top with calculator icon and "= result"
+  title. Enter copies result. Use a safe expression evaluator (no eval()).
+  Create src/utils/calculator.ts.
+- [ ] [P2] SCRN005 - Build File Search screen. List view showing mock file
+  system entries. Each item: file icon (different per type), filename, path as
+  subtitle, size + date accessories. Actions: Open, Show in Finder, Copy Path,
+  Quick Look. Demonstrate file type icons (folder, document, image, code).
+  Push via navigation from "File Search" command.
+- [ ] [P2] SCRN006 - Build Settings/Preferences screen. Pushed view with
+  tabbed/sectioned layout. Tabs: General (appearance toggle, hotkey display),
+  Extensions (list of installed extensions with enable/disable), Advanced
+  (export/import mock). Use Form components for settings fields. Push via ⌘,
+  shortcut.
+- [ ] [P3] SCRN007 - Build Extension Store screen. List view of mock extensions
+  with: extension icon, name, developer, download count accessory. Detail panel
+  shows description, screenshots placeholder, commands list, install button.
+  Search and category filter via List.Dropdown. Push from "Store" command.
+- [ ] [P3] SCRN008 - Build AI Chat interface. Separate view (not in main command
+  palette or styled differently). Chat bubbles: user messages (right-aligned),
+  AI responses (left-aligned, with markdown rendering). Input bar at bottom
+  with send button. Model selector dropdown. Mock streaming response effect
+  (typewriter). Push from "AI Chat" command.
+- [ ] [P3] SCRN009 - Build Notes editor screen. Markdown editor with toolbar
+  (bold, italic, strikethrough, code, heading, list, checklist). Split or
+  toggle between edit and preview modes. Notes list sidebar (or push from list).
+  Create src/components/notes/ with notes_editor.tsx, notes_list.tsx, and
+  .scss files. Push from "Notes" command.
+=== VISUAL POLISH & THEMES ===
+- [ ] [P2] THEME001 - Add light theme support. Create _tokens_light.scss with
+  light-mode color values. Add theme toggle mechanism (CSS class on root or
+  CSS custom properties). All components must respect current theme. Background
+  becomes light, text becomes dark, borders adjust. Test all components in
+  both themes.
+- [ ] [P2] THEME002 - Add compact mode. When no query is entered, show only
+  the search bar (hide list and action panel). List appears with fade-in when
+  user starts typing. Revert to compact when query is cleared. Toggle via
+  prop on CommandPalette. Animate height transition.
+- [ ] [P3] THEME003 - Add proper semantic color system. Create Color enum/object
+  matching Raycast's: Blue, Green, Magenta, Orange, Purple, Red, Yellow,
+  PrimaryText, SecondaryText. Each color auto-adapts to current theme. Use
+  CSS custom properties. Replace hardcoded hex in tag_colors.ts with semantic
+  colors. Create src/constants/colors.ts.
+=== KEYBOARD & INTERACTION ===
+- [ ] [P1] KBD001 - Implement global keyboard shortcut system. Create
+  src/hooks/use_keyboard_shortcuts.ts that manages all global shortcuts in one

+- [ ] [P2] KBD002 - Add favorite items support. ⌘⇧F toggles favorite on

+  star icon on favorited items. ⌘⌥↑/↓ reorders favorites.
+- [ ] [P2] KBD003 - Add ⌃N/⌃P alternative navigation. In addition to arrow
+  keys, support Ctrl+N (down) and Ctrl+P (up) for Emacs-style navigation in

+=== INFRASTRUCTURE ===
+- [ ] [P2] INFRA001 - Create proper extension/command registry system. Instead

+  commands with: name, icon, keywords, component (for push), aliases. Root

+- [ ] [P2] INFRA002 - Add localStorage persistence layer. Save and restore:

+- [ ] [P3] INFRA003 - Refactor App.tsx into smaller modules. Current App.tsx is

+  command handlers. Each view should be its own component file. App.tsx should

+=== TESTS ===
+- [ ] [P1000] TEST001 - Add visual regression tests with Playwright for all

+- [ ] [P1000] TEST002 - Add interaction tests for navigation stack: push view,

+- [ ] [P1000] TEST003 - Add interaction tests for ActionPanel.Section and

+- [ ] [P1000] TEST004 - Add unit tests for new hooks: useAlert, useHUD,

+- [ ] [P1000] TEST005 - Add visual tests for Clipboard History, Snippet

+- [ ] [P1000] TEST006 - Add visual tests for light theme. All existing
